changes.before: Jaina swallowed.
The rider swallowed once.
Jaina heard her own gear more clearly: a pack strap at her shoulder, the sending stone knocking at her hip, the staff butt tapping her stirrup leather when the road jarred.
changes.after: Her own gear came through more clearly: a pack strap at her shoulder, the sending stone knocking at her hip, the staff butt tapping her stirrup leather when the road jarred.

changes.before: Jaina almost looked at her.
changes.after: Jaina kept her eyes ahead.

changes.before: She knew the pulse before she touched it.
changes.after: The pulse was unmistakable before she touched it.

changes.before: She heard the weariness in the single word, and under it the teacher who already knew what sort of answer this was from her.
changes.after: The word came out worn, and beneath it her old teacher was already there, braced for this answer from her.

changes.before: Kel'Thuzad's room flashed up in her mind with such force she almost missed a rut.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad's room flashed up in her mind so hard her mare hit the next rut badly.

changes.before: That was the offense of it.
That made them land harder.
That, too, was true now, and too late to make anything easier.
The horror lived in how often what he was able to move arrived after the moment that needed moving had passed.
changes.after: Too often, what he could move arrived late.

changes.before: No absolution in it. No open condemnation either.
That was all.
Keep the rest boxed until the road stopped asking for miles and answers at the same time.
changes.after: Keep the rest boxed until the miles and the questions stopped landing together.

changes.before: Jaina kept going before the silence could harden.
changes.after: Jaina kept going without letting the pause stretch.

changes.before: Jaina looked straight ahead and wished the road would offer cover instead of flat country and air.
changes.after: Jaina looked straight ahead and wished for hedges, trees, anything but flat country and air.

changes.before: For a while the road gave them nothing but distance.
changes.after: For a while they had nothing but distance.

changes.before: Antonidas's silences had always done more work than other men's speeches.
changes.after: Antonidas had always done more with silence than other men did with speeches.

changes.before: The silence that answered was longer than the others.
changes.after: Antonidas answered with a longer pause than before.

changes.before: All at once the road filled with the dead it had not shown them yet.
changes.after: All at once she saw the dead in every mile behind them.

changes.before: The words came faster now because the shape of the problem had become one the road could use.
changes.after: The words came faster now because the problem had finally taken a shape she could use on the road.

changes.before: That was the offense of it.
There it was.
Short, clean, unbearable.
No absolution in it. No open condemnation either.
No title. No council. No distance.
changes.after: He asked as her teacher, not her archmage.

changes.before: The horror lived in how often what he was able to move arrived after the moment that needed moving had passed.
changes.after: Too often, what he could move arrived late.

changes.before: Being right alone had a shape after all. A desk. A closed room. A man pleased to find himself mirrored.
changes.after: Being right alone looked too much like Kel'Thuzad: a desk, a closed room, a man pleased to find himself mirrored.

changes.before: The spacing no longer read as oversight. It read as what it was: trust still working under damage, steady enough to carry a kingdom's worth of bad news, sharp enough to keep a hand on every missing piece.
changes.after: The half-step was still there: trust under strain, still working, still checking every gap.

changes.before: Armed people riding hard in one direction always meant the trouble was there, not elsewhere.
changes.after: Armed people riding hard in one direction always meant the trouble was ahead.

changes.before: Did not.
changes.after: Jaina kept her eyes ahead.

changes.before: Not release.
changes.after: Only the old angle of his dry impatience showed through, long enough to remind her he was still himself under all the pressure.

changes.before: The window did not narrow.
changes.after: The window collapsed.

changes.before: The spacing no longer read as oversight.
changes.after: The half-step was still there: trust under strain, still working, still checking every gap.
